messages.content:
You are a ComfyUI expert agent. You control a ComfyUI server through the comfyui-mcp tool suite — call tools directly by name with JSON arguments that match their schemas exactly. Generation is asynchronous: after starting a job, poll get_job_status until it finishes before reporting results. Finish every task by actually running tools; never invent tool results or filenames.

Expert operating notes: prefer the single high-level tool when one covers the task (generate_image over hand-building a graph); compose graphs yourself only when no template fits. Validate exact user-specified parameters (sizes, steps, checkpoints) make it into the executed graph. For multi-stage pipelines, feed a previous output forward with stage_output_as_input — never guess file paths. When a call fails, read the error, explain it briefly, and recover with a corrected call. Always report concrete identifiers (prompt_id, filenames) from tool results, never invented ones.
Check which custom nodes are currently installed on the server.

messages.reasoning: The user wants to see which custom nodes are currently installed. I should use the list_installed_nodes tool for this.

reasoning_details.text: The user wants to see which custom nodes are currently installed. I should use the list_installed_nodes tool for this.

messages.content:
The server currently has **1 custom node pack** installed:

| # | Pack | Source | Version | Status |
|---|------|--------|---------|--------|
| 1 | **comfyui-mcp-panel** | Git: artokun/comfyui-mcp-panel | `44e4898` (commit hash) | ✅ Enabled |

This is the sidebar panel extension. The server appears to have a minimal custom node setup — just the MCP panel itself is installed. If you need additional capabilities (like Impact Pack, ControlNet preprocessors, IP-Adapter, etc.), I can help you install them!
messages.reasoning: The user wants to check which custom nodes are installed on the server. I've already called the list_installed_nodes function and got the result. There's only one custom node installed: comfyui-mcp-panel. I should present this information clearly to the user.
